title: Add namespace-scoped RBAC and namespace flag
summary: Review #2 covers the changes addressing sourcery-ai[bot] feedback: (1) ManagerConfig.Namespace field added so callers can opt into namespace scoping programmatically without CLI flags, with CLI flag defaulting to cfg.Namespace and overriding when explicitly set; (2) RBAC rules extracted from duplicated inline blocks in clusterrole.yaml and role.yaml into a shared named template keystone-operator.rbacRules in _helpers.tpl, eliminating drift risk. All verification passes: 9/9 Go tests, 69/69 Helm tests, helm lint clean for both configs, helm template output correct for both modes, go vet clean.

tests_checklist.item: Go unit tests pass (9/9 including new TestManagerConfig_validate_validWithNamespace)
Helm unittest tests pass (69/69 across 9 suites)
helm lint passes for default and namespace-scoped configurations
helm template with defaults produces ClusterRole (no Role)
helm template with rbac.namespaceScoped=true produces Role (no ClusterRole)
Edge cases covered: empty namespace = cluster-wide, non-empty = scoped
New Namespace field on ManagerConfig has dedicated validation test

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (flag registration style, ManagerConfig struct conventions)
cacheOptions helper is pure function with clear single responsibility
Helm shared partial eliminates code duplication between clusterrole.yaml and role.yaml
Feature IDs present in comments and docstrings (CC-0043)
No dead code or unnecessary changes

security_checklist.item: No secrets or credentials in changes
RBAC rules are identical between Role and ClusterRole (shared partial)
No input validation bypass (namespace flag is optional string)
No path traversal or injection vectors

architecture_checklist.item: ManagerConfig.Namespace exposes config through both CLI and programmatic surfaces
CLI flag defaults to cfg.Namespace, overrides when explicitly set — clean precedence model
Shared RBAC rules partial prevents drift between Role and ClusterRole

dry_yagni_checklist.item: RBAC rules extracted to shared partial (DRY)
No speculative features beyond what was requested

fail_fast_checklist.item: ManagerConfig.validate() catches nil Scheme and empty LeaderElectionID early
cacheOptions handles empty namespace gracefully (nil DefaultNamespaces)

defensive_checklist.item: Empty namespace string correctly means cluster-wide (no DefaultNamespaces set)
Flag precedence documented: CLI --namespace overrides ManagerConfig.Namespace
